reasoning: M1 Money Supply (NSA) exhibits strong seasonality: it peaks in early January and drops significantly through February before rising again in March/April due to tax season. The current data sequence shows this expected post-holiday drop already beginning, with M1 falling from 19346.7 (Jan 5) to 19092.6 (Jan 26). By February 8, the most recent available data will reflect late January/early February, which will be further down the seasonal trough. By March 3, the latest data will reflect late February, which historically remains below late January levels before the bulk of tax refunds hit. The FRED tool's linear trend overestimates the probability of an increase because it fits an upward line on a period (July to Jan) that naturally trends upward due to seasonality. Relying purely on the seasonal behavior, M1 is highly likely to be lower on both resolution dates compared to early February.
